PLAINTIFF DAVID MILLSTEIN's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 And Entry Of Judgment

Plaintiff David Millstein's motion to enforce settlement agreement and for entry of judgment is granted. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcripts)

Order and Judgment are signed.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, By Defendants Katherine Papageorge And Papageorgiou, Inc.
Off Calendar Feb-24-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Gerald Richelson of the law offices of Millstein & Associates (415.348.0348) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Stella Fey Epling of the law offices of North Beach Law (415.433.1448) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

Judge Lam discloses that as a former public defender, he previously appeared opposite of Mr. Millstein and is an acquaintance of Mr. Millstein. Judge Lam does not believe that these prior interactions are a basis for recusal as a reasonable person would not doubt his impartiality. No objection by either side, the parties stipulate that the hearing may proceed.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS KATHERINE PAPAGEORGE AND PAPAGEORGIOU, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED in its entirety. Ms. Papageorge and Papageorgiou, Inc. have not satisfied their initial burden. The facts stated in their separate statement do not address the elements of a common count or indicate they are not indebted to Mr. Millstein regardless of whether a written fee agreement existed. Even if Defendants had maintained their initial burden, a triable issue exists as to whether Mr. Millstein entered an oral fee agreement with Ms. Papageorge and Papageorgiou, Inc. (See Response to UMF 11; Millstein Declaration, 11-12.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DAVID MILLSTEIN Motion To Take Motion For Summary Judgment Off Calendar is denied. The November 8, 2016 order does not bar the filing of a summary judgment motion. Judge Stewart's interlineations related to discovery. The court has "jurisdiction" to hear the motion for summary judgment because the time limitations of CCP 437c are triggered off of the present trial date. If plaintiff needs further discovery to oppose the summary judgment motion, then plaintiff should comply with the provisions of CCP 437c(h). The court does not find this motion to be frivolous within the meaning of CCP 128.5. Order signed. Judge: A.
